downside_protection.details: Net debt position: $-20,968,000,000; Weak liquidity (current ratio 1.18); Very low leverage (D/E 0.28); Positive and improving/stable FCF

valuation.details: Borderline value: 3.2% FCF yield; Asset-light: Avg capex 1.1% of revenue

double_potential.details: Strong revenue trajectory (28.9%); Strong FCF growth (209.0%)

macro_context.market_sentiment: Broadly bullish on May 1, 2026: S&P 500 pushed above 7,200 to a fresh all-time high (+0.6%), Nasdaq Composite +1.1% (also a new ATH), driven by Apple's strong Q1 earnings beat. April was the best monthly performance for both indices since 2020, and tech stocks are leading the rally.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held rates at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, 2026 amid an unusual 4-member dissent — Miran favored a cut, while Hammack, Kashkari and Logan opposed the easing-bias language in the statement. Powell announced he will remain on the Board of Governors after his chairmanship ends; market interprets hold as cautious given elevated energy-driven inflation.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East conflict ongoing with Strait of Hormuz risk keeping energy prices elevated and contributing to ~4.4% global headline inflation (IMF). Iran sent a new peace proposal via Pakistani mediators on May 1, lifting market sentiment. U.S.-Venezuela tensions and NATO posture in Greenland add secondary uncertainty. Tariff uncertainty persists heading into midterm elections.
key_events: Amazon Ads Upfront 2026 — May 11 at Beacon Theatre, NYC (ad-tech showcase)
Ongoing Iran-U.S. nuclear/peace negotiations — next update from Pakistani mediators
Next FOMC meeting — June 2026 (no cut currently priced in given elevated inflation)
U.S. midterm elections in November 2026 creating policy uncertainty

ticker_news.headline: Amazon smashes Q1 2026 earnings — EPS $2.78 vs $1.63 estimate; revenue $181.5B; AWS +28% YoY to $37.6B
Anthropic commits $100B+ over 10 years to AWS cloud infrastructure in expanded partnership
OpenAI shifts aggressively from Microsoft Azure to Amazon AWS; Microsoft loses exclusive IP/API cloud deal
Meta signs AWS Graviton5 AI-workload deal expected to be worth billions of dollars
Amazon up 27% in one month — best-performing Magnificent Seven stock in 2026, shares rerated toward all-time highs

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Post-Q1 beat: Robert W. Baird raised PT $285→$300 (Outperform); Oppenheimer raised PT $275→$320 (Outperform); Mizuho raised to Street-high $325 (Buy); BMO raised to $315; UBS raised to $304. DA Davidson had cut PT from $300→$175 on Feb 6, 2026 (contrarian downgrade). Strong Buy consensus from 43-47 analysts; PT range $240–$370.

web_context.competitor_activity: AWS posted +28% growth in Q1 — fastest in 15 quarters — versus Azure +40% and Google Cloud +63% (from a smaller base). AWS retains ~31% global cloud market share vs Azure ~24% and Google Cloud ~12%. OpenAI's departure from Microsoft exclusive toward AWS is a meaningful share-shift catalyst. Big Tech hyperscalers collectively plan $725B capex in 2026 (+77% YoY), with Amazon guiding ~$200B. Microsoft remains a strong Azure competitor with deep enterprise integration; Google Cloud is betting on AI-first enterprise. In e-commerce, Walmart continues to close the gap but Amazon's logistics moat remains large.
web_context.earnings_info: Q1 2026 reported April 29, 2026: EPS $2.78 (beat $1.63 estimate by $1.15), revenue $181.5B. Next earnings (Q2 2026) expected ~July 30, 2026 after market close (date unconfirmed). Q2 EPS and revenue consensus estimates not yet widely published; revenue guidance from Amazon implied ~$188.9B for Q2.
